body.title: Codebase annotator: delta update on ticket close via git diff
body.result: file_paths delta mode in annotator; _annotator_delta_update in cc_queue.py close hook; timestamps advance for touched nodes; 6 tests
body.intention: I intend that codebase tree nodes are re-annotated on ticket close for only the files touched, keeping the tree fresh without nightly latency.
body.description:
On ticket close, Classifier device receives score() call with files_actually_touched. Run git diff --name-only for the ticket's commits, re-annotate only those files, update palace.codebase nodes. This is the primary freshness mechanism; nightly sweep is catchall for out-of-band changes.

**Affected files:** devices/classifier/annotator.py (delta method), cc_queue.py or ticket close hook (trigger point)
**Design rules:** log every interface crossing; non-fatal — if annotator fails, ticket closes successfully regardless
**Scope boundary:** In scope: delta re-annotation on close + score() precision/recall logging. Out of scope: contention detection, multi-project.
**Completion criteria:** T-annotator-delta-update is CLOSED; ticket close triggers re-annotation of touched files; palace node updated_at timestamp advances for those files; annotator failure does not block ticket close.
